isotopes.application: The most abundant nuclide in the universe; fuel for stellar fusion and the basis of all hydrogen chemistry, including water.
Deuterium — used as a tracer in chemistry/biology (NMR solvents), as a moderator in heavy-water (CANDU) reactors, and as fuel for fusion research.
Tritium — used in self-luminous exit signs and watch dials, as a tracer in biomedical/hydrological research, and as fusion fuel (with deuterium) in experimental reactors and thermonuclear weapons.

A short-lived alpha/beta emitter from the thorium decay chain, used in targeted alpha therapy research.
Used in targeted alpha-particle therapy research for cancer (e.g. paired with antibodies for leukemia treatment).
